chapter_context.summary: David honors his covenant with Jonathan by showing kindness to Jonathan's lame son Mephibosheth, restoring his land and inviting him to eat at his table.

outline.text: David Inquires for Descendants of Saul to Show Kindness
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David Inquires for Descendants of Saul to Show Kindness (9:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: Mephibosheth Brought to David; Receives Saul's Land
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Mephibosheth Brought to David; Receives Saul's Land (9:5-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: Ziba Appointed to Farm the Land; Mephibosheth Eats at King's Table
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Ziba Appointed to Farm the Land; Mephibosheth Eats at King's Table (9:9-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

key_themes.title: The Davidic Covenant
key_themes.description: God's unconditional promise of an eternal dynasty, ultimately pointing forward to the Messiah.

key_themes.title: Sin and Consequences
key_themes.description: While God offers complete forgiveness for sincere repentance, the earthly consequences of sin remain.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 9 demonstrate the grace of God in the midst of human failure?
What lessons about leadership and accountability can you apply to your own life from this chapter?

cultural_historical.title: Jerusalem as Capital
cultural_historical.text: By capturing Jerusalem, a neutral city on the border of Judah and Benjamin, David strategically united the fractured northern and southern tribes under a single capital.
